{"text":"In India's latest national budget, motorcycles with engines over 350 CC capacity would fall under higher tax rate, so the domestic indian market is going to take some time to adjust to this. Not that there is any sudden reduction in demand for 350+ capacity bikes in India, but companies are going to hold their horses back a little when it comes to launching new platforms using their domestic brand names (at least until the spending capacity of Indian consumers normalizes again), rather they would rely on foreign brand partners or acquisitions to launch newer platforms with higher capacities with joint production and/or sale in India and for Indian export markets like Africa and South America. Since this market is still small compared to India's domestic market, i would not expect a a 600cc or 750cc mid bikes coming below the price of R130,000 at the moment, because economies of scale would not justify it. However, in the next 3-5 years, when Indian spending capacity crosses a certain threshold (say $3500 per capita gdp), you would see a whole new variety in mid size bike segments, maybe at an even lower price point."}

"},{"text":"People think Africa, India and Latin America would be safe bets in the next two decades for investment. But how are you going to solve the dropping fertility rates in those countries? {"text":"This video is made with an inherent bias. India doesn't need to show off economic growth with skyscrapers. 2-5 FSI for cities is more than enough. Increasing population density also increases burden on existing infrastructure like transportation, water, electricity, sanitation, parking space etc. Taller the building, more are its requirements. Traditional European cities do not even consider building skyscrapers cause it doesn't fit the cities natural aesthetics. They limit these skyscrapers to a business district. India too has ancient cities with its own natural aesthetics hidden under all the dust of construction, illegal billboards, wiring etc. We just need to reorganize our cities in a better way. Don't need to blindly follow western definition of modern city. Look at Burj Khalifa, looks beautiful, but doesn't even have proper sanitation. Look at China, half of their Skyscrapers are empty."}
